headlines.title: 5 vehicles stolen from Alameda County parking garage in Oakland
headlines.description: Five vehicles were stolen from an Alameda County parking garage in Oakland Sunday morning, according to authorities. The sheriff’s office said personnel discovered that the Alameda County Parking Garage at 165 13th Street in Oakland was burglarized at 7:16 a.m., and five vehicles were stolen, including Alameda County vehicles and a sheriff’s office vehicle. One of the stolen vehicles was a rental car, the sheriff’s office said. Officials said investigators initially recovered four vehicles using automated license plate reader cameras, and they were looking for the fifth. By Sunday evening, authorities told NBC Bay Area the last outstanding vehicle was found. Sergeant Roberto Morales, Public Information Officer with the Alameda County Sheriff’s Office said, “the investigation is still going to continue.” Morales added, “We’re going to try and figure out what happened, probably set up looking into our security protocols, harden the site, see what happened, how did this happen, and trying to find out who did this, why they did this.” Morales said that the thief or theives forced entry into the gararge. Several Alameda County Supervisors told NBC Bay Area Sunday they are awaiting more information abotu this incident, but plan to look into what happened. EDITOR’S NOTE: Sheriff’s officials initially reported five sheriff’s office vehicles were stolen, but they later clarified that four of them were county vehicles and at least one was a sheriff’s office unmarked vehicle.

headlines.title: Bay Area readies for World Cup as kickoff nears
headlines.description: With the FIFA World Cup set to begin in just days, changes are already underway at Levi’s Stadium in Santa Clara. During the tournament, the home of the 49ers will be known as San Francisco Bay Area Stadium, in accordance with FIFA sponsorship rules. Signage at the stadium has already been covered as part of the transition. The change has also appeared online. Apple Maps now lists the venue as San Francisco Bay Area Stadium to help visitors attending matches during the tournament. The World Cup will be played across the United States, Mexico and Canada. The tournament begins Thursday in Mexico City, while the Bay Area’s first match is scheduled for Saturday, when Qatar faces Switzerland. Meanwhile, Team Paraguay arrived in the Bay Area over the weekend. The team landed at San Jose Mineta International Airport and will use San Jose State University as its base camp during the tournament. Paraguay’s first match is scheduled for Friday in Los Angeles against the U.S. men’s national team. NBC Bay Area’s Bob Redell has more the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: Bay Area fire officials warn fire danger is only getting worse
headlines.description: Sunday was a busy day for firefighters across the Bay Area, and fire officials said the fire danger is only getting worse. Firefighters from San Jose to Santa Rosa were chasing grass fires since Sunday morning. San Jose firefighters were able to contain a fire near Communications Hill to just under 8 acres, where four different fire starts converged into one larger grass fire. The cause is under investigation. In the North Bay, Santa Rosa fire crews quickly attacked another grass fire, and stopped it from spreading to fewer than 2 acres. With temperatures getting up into the 90s and even approaching triple digits in some areas later this week, fire agencies statewide are ramping up training and getting that equipment ready. Thom Jensen has the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: Incident involving suspect with a knife closes Hwy. 101 in San Jose
headlines.description: An incident involving a suspect armed with a knife prompted the temporary closure of northbound Highway 101 at Hellyer Avenue on Sunday afternoon, according to authorities. San Jose police said officers responded to the 4200 block of Dove Road for a report of a trespassing with a person with a knife. At the scene, officers found the suspect acting erratic and still armed with the knife, according to authorities. Police said the suspect was taken into custody shortly before 3:30 p.m. Officials said the incident occurred near the freeway, which led to CHP temporarily closing at least a lane of northbound Highway 101 at Hellyer Avenue.

headlines.title: San Jose fire crews put out vegetation fire near Communications Hill
headlines.description: Fire crews in San Jose put out a vegetation fire Sunday afternoon that burned over 7 acres, the San Jose Fire Department said. Four separate fires converged into one in the area of Hillsdale Avenue and Communications Hill Boulevard, the Fire Department said. The call came in at about 2:20 p.m. and was under control by 4:30 p.m. A total of about 7.63 acres burned and there were no injuries. The cause of the fire is under investigation.

headlines.title: Oakland mayor announces she is running for reelection
headlines.description: Oakland’s mayor announced Saturday she is officially running for reelection. In a message posted on social media, Barbara Lee said her work is not finished, and she wants to keep fighting for a comprehensive approach to homelessness, more affordable housing, public safety, good paying jobs, and safe, clean neighborhoods. She also outlined some of her accomplishments. Lee took credit for lowering crime rates, modeling gun violence reduction, cutting homelessness in half, and breaking ground on affordable housing units.

headlines.title: San Francisco General Hospital fined over deadly stabbing of staff member
headlines.description: The Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital responded to its recent fine from Cal/OSHA following the deadly stabbing of a hospital staff member. In December, a social worker was stabbed to the death at the hospital. Now, Cal/OSHA has ordered San Francisco General to pay roughly $130,000 for serious workplace violence-prevention violations. In a report, investigators accused the hospital of being “utterly unprepared to deal with workplace violence” when the worker was attacked. San Francisco Dec 16, 2025 San Francisco Dec 8, 2025 San Francisco Dec 6, 2025 San Francisco’s Health Department says it took immediate action after the killing. On Sunday, it released a lengthy statement which reads, in part: “Day in and day out our frontline healthcare workers deliver critical, life-saving care under demanding conditions and they deserve to do so safely, with the protection and support they need. Following the tragic death of our colleague Alberto Rangel at Ward 86 at Zuckerberg San Francisco General Hospital (ZSFG) on December 4, 2025, the San Francisco Department of Public Health took immediate action. The department convened a 100-person team of SFDPH and University of California San Francisco staff, who made rapid improvements to better protect staff and patients, while also advancing long-term structural changes. This involved an internal comprehensive assessment of safety, not only at ZSFG, but throughout the entire SFDPH system of care. The voices of SFDPH and UCSF caregivers, combined with expert security and operational analysis, shaped the assessment and have guidedlasting, systemwide improvements to workplace safety. We will continue to invest in the safety of every person who walks through our doors. The City has committed $15 million annually and $7.5 million in one-time infrastructureimprovements to support this effort. Significant funding is dedicated to workplace safety training and deescalation training for staff. The department welcomed and fully cooperated with a thorough review from Cal/OSHA. The findings are in line with our own rigorous internal assessment. The vast majority of their findings to overall safety and security have been addressed, some within days of the incident. Countless additional security measures have been initiated or expedited to strengthen workplace safety, including enhanced physical security measures, expanded security staffing, increased crisis prevention and response training, and a fundamental change in security structure governance to better connect leadership to frontline staff and their concerns. The safety for our staff, our patients, and our community is not negotiable, and we will continue to keep staff and patients safe with a strengthened and modernized approach to safety and security.”

headlines.title: Suspects steal thousands of dollars' worth of merchandise from Brentwood store
headlines.description: Thieves hit a store in Brentwood Saturday just before closing time, taking thousands of dollars in merchandise. It happened at the Card Lab, a Pokémon and sports collectible store. Its owner, Ronnie Hackett, said three people in dark clothing ran right before closing time and jumped on the counter and directly toward the shelf with the high-ticket booster boxes containing 36 packs of Pokémon cards and other items throwing them into a bag. He believes the cost of the items was about $15,000. He says the employee had done some end of the day protocol putting some things upstairs and was going downstairs when she saw people jump over the counter. Hackett says he did what she felt was the right thing and ran back upstairs and locked herself in a break room while the thieves were inside. They were out the door quickly, Brentwood police were called, and so was Hackett, who came as quickly as he could. The employee wasn’t hurt, but Hackett says he was also distraught this happened to an employee. “At the end of the day, I want these guys to be found. Granted, we may or may not get our stuff back, but there has got be consequences for doing what they’re doing. I would really hate for them to continue to keep doing this, so bringing the awareness is important for me,” Hackett said. Hackett says the Card Lab is closed for the next couple days while they figure things out. Meantime, police say, based on preliminary information, this is being investigated as grand theft. It’s an ongoing investigation and they’re asking anyone with information to contact Brentwood police.

headlines.title: Preliminary 3.6 magnitude earthquake strikes near Alamo
headlines.description: A preliminary 3.6 magnitude earthquake struck near Alamo at 8:37 a.m. Sunday, according to the United States Geological Survey. The epicenter was recorded at 1.1 miles southwest of Alamo. No damages or injuries have been reported. This is a developing story. Details may change as more information becomes available. Stay tuned for updates. BAY AREA QUAKE CENTRAL Bay Area Quake Tracker, the latest earthquake stories, extensive quake prep checklists, videos and many other disaster preparedness resources all in one place: NBCBAYAREA.COM/QUAKES

headlines.title: Police identify victim in deadly shooting after graduation ceremony in Fairfield
headlines.description: On Saturday, police identified the 18-year-old shot and killed after a high school graduation ceremony in Fairfield. Police said 18-year-old Jamario Baker was the victim of the deadly shooting that occurred following Sem Yeto High School’s graduation ceremony at the Fairfield High School campus on Wednesday evening. According to police, three other people were also shot, including an 11-year-old child and two others in their 20s. “Our thoughts and condolences are with his family, friends, and all those affected during this difficult time,” police said in a statement. “The investigation remains active, and there are currently no suspects in custody. Detectives are working diligently to follow all available leads and are committed to bringing those responsible for this senseless act of violence to justice.” Fairfield Jun 5 Fairfield Jun 4 Fairfield Jun 3 Teen dies in shooting after high school graduation ceremony in Fairfield

headlines.title: Fairfield community gathers to heal following shooting after graduation ceremony
headlines.description: On Saturday, the Fairfield community gathered in the parking lot of Fairfield High School to heal following Wednesday’s deadly shooting after a graduation ceremony. The gathering was organized by the Tri-City NAACP and the local community group Broken by Violence. The gathering at Civic Center Park was organizer before authorities identified the 18-year-old killed in the shooting. Those who came to the gathering say this was not about personally knowing the victim, but about helping a community process a traumatic event. “Right now there is nobody being apprehended for this heinous crime, community has to be even more diligent in standing with this family,” said Broken by Violence Executive Director Ebony Antoine. Fairfield Jun 6 Fairfield Jun 5 Fairfield Jun 4 Police say the shooting happened in the parking lot of Fairfield Highafter a graduation ceremony for Sem Yeto Continuation High School. Four people were shot, with baker dying from his injuries. The three survivors, including an 11-year-old, are currently in stable condition. Police say the shooting remains under investigation. And as of Saturday, no suspect has been found.

headlines.title: Pacifica Municipal Pier remains closed as damage gets worse
headlines.description: Along the coast, Pacifica has proclaimed a local emergency due to damage to the Pacifica Municipal Pier. The city noticed cracking, separation and displacement of the concrete walkway and structural area near the abutment and Chit Chat Cafe. The city shut down the pier on Thursday after staff noticed cracking separation and displacement of the concrete walkway. But now, it seems the damage is getting worse. Dave, a visitor, has been to the pier many times before over the years. “Lot people enjoy coming out here I been out here back when it was open all the way to the end and it was just a really cool experience,” he said. But now, he can’t walk out on the pier at all. “I was surprised to see what it did over night from the initial cracks and how big it got it so it’s just sad,” Dave said. Along with the pier, Chit Chat Cafe is also closed. Its owners posted on social media that they believe the cafe may have to be demolished. Now, flowers sit outside the establishment, and an online fundraiser for it has so far raised thousands of dollars. Another store owner in town says his business has been steady. “We have no control over of it at least our neighborhood is very very good to us they are very focused,” said Jack with the Sharp Park Market. The pier will remain closed while the city’s structual engineer does an assessments to determine next steps.
